mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within one month signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in real residential construction starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when both series decline simultaneously, or single-series models outperform the interaction term in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, rising industrial production reflects pent-up demand fulfillment and capacity utilization improvements, which positively drive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains fail to correlate with positive equity returns during recovery episodes, or the relationship reverses to negative during high-inflation recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding the historical median signals robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth surprises consistently fail to move equity prices, or the forward return differential between above-median and below-median GDP growth periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0 percent, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally persistently after the rate threshold is breached, or forward returns show no significant difference between periods above and below the 2.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal does not outperform single-series models in predicting 20-day returns, or positive equity returns occur despite contradictory labor data (e.g., payroll growth with rising unemployment).

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, rising consumer credit outstanding indicates peak leverage and impending demand exhaustion, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consumer credit growth fails to precede equity drawdowns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances.
